textByHash.67cabf2b7a04f57a43d74eefe9796f55078c4372: So, you are the outerlander whom Hamal said would look for Asleif.
textByHash.dae9a86eafc25d38cab91a84ba648a538e3d1dbc: You don't even know her name!
textByHash.fd3021f2a41406916761e11fcc6fe27aa244196f: I am sorry outerlander, I have not spoken with your kind for a long time and I'm not feeling so well. You see, I was set to marry Asleif, the most beautiful woman ever to grace this world.
textByHash.2c2bc451ddd1ff8cb51fac0a0c1edd3c0d55b4ba: She used to come here every day, you know, whenever our camp was set up in the vicinity. She would come to sing, her voice reflecting on the mountains and falling into the pool like sparkling silver. Sometimes I think I can hear her singing, but when I follow the sound around the pool I just end up here again.
textByHash.06363c13d06c9f075ac4a3bbd1dfd071831a3f01: We were supposed to meet here, at the tree by the pool. I think if I wait long enough maybe she will show up again.
textByHash.bd1e0a1d4f55bda05ae828ce81bdc33d1ccf732e: If I knew I wouldn't be standing here, would I?
textByHash.737f6fa4aa1c31a498313563be38652f09438a5b: Yet you still use magic, I assume, and have little actual knowledge of the earth and the rock that you stand on every day.
textByHash.ad0707cb9da0e1b3a0f6393b3d42c72bbdd4d5f2: Then you are an ignorant, an insult to the gods... and an outerlander.
textByHash.8d514f541647632001400034063bc664086dfe8a: Really? You found her? Where is she, tell me!
textByHash.b99dd7eba5b2df071e51a6a0b9aa0fb78e40bd7b: What? Do not say such things! Tell me where you saw her!
textByHash.472768672d2cb249adf8b733dac06ae0db923d61: That island is bare! There's no one there! Do not try to deceive me, outerlander, she is not dead and you did not talk to her!
textByHash.de4661a3330096d67cc7e2339cc6efa25d02f8d2: Do not say such things, outerlander! She is not dead, merely missing.
textByHash.3a8b3bf65d3e3ce2b269bc2ce216d917b1d400fc: She'll show up again... yes, yes, I'm sure of it.
textByHash.df8e39e4ab544e160f08c621df8ef061f60fe750: Yes, I know... well, that doesn't mean she can't still come back, right?
textByHash.d9d830ba84d645f7f10b9dfe63f5322bc0b335fb: I do not want to see any of the corpses you're carrying around with you! Begone!
textByHash.dfc7e0fb882e85e43ba7167259a09d7a8ce62688: I heard the news from Hamal, outerlander. Alas, I have known this had to be so for a long time, but Hamal just didn't want to listen.
textByHash.8f9ccbcf9204fbfe23ec39f2b3fa574ec8c82abb: I'm not altogether sure... I do have one thing that may be appropriate.
textByHash.ef7b87eaf3f8a6d3aaa00c0a19dad4860f76e0e0: I was going to give her a necklace, when we were supposed to meet here many years ago.
textByHash.47777bd96cb9eb30181610c4974ff9b1c665555e: I still have it, and since it was a gift for Asleif, technically it's hers.
textByHash.6e148e547796d236b406c136d263d1b941542089: Take it, outerlander, and bury it with her. It will be my parting gift.
textByHash.7cb2e9ab818829f4b14bac7ec781944d9c88dc19: Oh yes... she is my only love, you see, and will be so forever.
textByHash.cbc76b775afa7e3a8af955da562691f85bb2513b: I do not expect you to understand such things. But perhaps you will find your own love one day, and then you will remember these words.
textByHash.94272912845199f5fac5be83eb09bff90372c89c: It's no use, I'm done for!
textByHash.ee1dfdc9fb54c5db3b403305f7a5fa373badabc8: It is most unusual, outerlander, that you were able to help us in this manner. But you have my thanks.
textByHash.107896c9f89c92a1ff586cdc344a8241f27c9409: Please, now, leave me alone for awhile while I watch the rippling of the water.
